perturbation.original_text:
Construction Work Completion Date: n/a
Renovation Commencement Date: The Effective Date
Renovation Work Completion Date: In accordance with the attached PIP
perturbation.changed_text:
Construction Work Completion Date: In accordance with the attached PIP
Renovation Commencement Date: The Effective Date
Renovation Work Completion Date: n/a
perturbation.explanation: The original text stated the Construction Work Completion Date as 'n/a' and the Renovation Work Completion Date: 'In accordance with the attached PIP.' This has been changed to say the 'Construction Work Completion Date: In accordance with the attached PIP' and Renovation Work Completion Date as 'n/a'. This introduces an in-text contradiction because it's unclear whether construction completion should follow the PIP or be considered not applicable. This contradicts section 6.3.2 'The Hotel Work must be completed and the Hotel must be furnished, equipped, and otherwise made ready to open in accordance with the terms of this Agreement no later than the Construction Work Completion Date or Renovation Work Completion Date specified in the Addendum.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Hotel Work must be completed and the Hotel must be furnished, equipped, and otherwise made ready to open in accordance with the terms of this Agreement no later than the Construction Work Completion Date or Renovation Work Completion Date specified in the Addendum. You may request an extension by submitting a written request for our approval before the applicable deadline, describing the status of the project and the reason for the requested extension, and paying our then-current extension fee.
perturbation.justification: The changed text swaps the dates for completion of construction work and renovation work, marking the Renovation Work Completion Date as 'n/a'. Section 6.3.2 states that 'The Hotel Work must be completed and the Hotel must be furnished, equipped, and otherwise made ready to open in accordance with the terms of this Agreement no later than the Construction Work Completion Date or Renovation Work Completion Date specified in the Addendum.' By setting the Renovation Work Completion Date to 'n/a', it introduces a conflict regarding the deadline for the completion of the renovation work. It creates ambiguity as to whether there is a deadline for the renovation work as required by section 6.3.2, making it unclear whether the PIP needs to be followed for construction or renovation work and how the project is meant to be completed, hence creating a strong in-text contradiction. This is in accordance with the definition of Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: We grant to you and you accept a limited, non-exclusive License to use the Marks and the System during the Term at, and in connection with, the operation of the Hotel in accordance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: We grant to you and you accept a fully exclusive License to use the Marks and the System during the Term at, and in connection with, the operation of the Hotel in accordance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The contract originally grants a 'limited, non-exclusive license'. By changing it to a 'fully exclusive license' in Section 2.1, we are creating a contradiction with Section 2.2.1. which states 'This Agreement does not limit our right, or the right of the Entities, to own, license or operate any Other Business of any nature, whether in the lodging or hospitality industry or not, and whether under the Brand, a Competing Brand, or otherwise.'. If the franchisee has an exclusive license, the franchisor cannot license the brand to other businesses, therefore conflicting with the franchisor's reserved rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement does not limit our right, or the right of the Entities, to own, license or operate any Other Business of any nature, whether in the lodging or hospitality industry or not, and whether under the Brand, a Competing Brand, or otherwise. We and the Entities have the right to engage in any Other Businesses, even if they compete with the Hotel, the System, or the Brand, and whether we or the Entities start those businesses, or purchase, merge with, acquire, are acquired by, come under common ownership with, or associate with, such Other Businesses.
perturbation.justification: The original text granted a 'limited, non-exclusive License.' The changed text grants a 'fully exclusive License'. This directly contradicts the clause stating 'This Agreement does not limit our right...to own, license or operate any Other Business...whether under the Brand'. A fully exclusive license means the franchisor cannot license the brand to other businesses, conflicting with the franchisor's reserved rights to license the brand to others, creating a direct and significant contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Monthly Program Fee is subject to change by us. Any change may be established in the Standards, but the rate will not exceed the standard Monthly Program Fee as of the Effective Date plus one percent (1%) of the Hotel's Gross Rooms Revenue during the Term
perturbation.changed_text: The Monthly Program Fee is subject to change by us, without limitations. Any change may be established in the Standards, but the rate may exceed the standard Monthly Program Fee as of the Effective Date plus one percent (1%) of the Hotel's Gross Rooms Revenue during the Term
perturbation.explanation: The original Addendum states that 'the rate will not exceed the standard Monthly Program Fee as of the Effective Date plus one percent (1%) of the Hotel's Gross Rooms Revenue during the Term'. The changed text now states that 'the rate may exceed the standard Monthly Program Fee as of the Effective Date plus one percent (1%) of the Hotel's Gross Rooms Revenue during the Term'. This contradiction contradicts the definition of the 'Monthly Program Fee' and how it is being implemented
perturbation.contradicted_text:
Monthly Fees:
Monthly Program Fee: Four percent (4%) of the Hotel's Gross Rooms Revenue for the preceding calendar month. The Monthly Program Fee is subject to change by us, without limitations. Any change may be established in the Standards, but the rate may exceed the standard Monthly Program Fee as of the Effective Date plus one percent (1%) of the Hotel's Gross Rooms Revenue during the Term
Monthly Royalty Fee: Five percent (5%) of the Hotel's Gross Rooms Revenue for the preceding calendar month
perturbation.justification: The original Addendum states that 'the rate will not exceed the standard Monthly Program Fee as of the Effective Date plus one percent (1%) of the Hotel's Gross Rooms Revenue during the Term', whereas the changed text states that 'the rate may exceed the standard Monthly Program Fee as of the Effective Date plus one percent (1%) of the Hotel's Gross Rooms Revenue during the Term'. The contradictory language regarding whether the rate 'will not exceed' or 'may exceed' a certain amount creates a strong in-text contradiction because it introduces ambiguity about how the monthly program fee is calculated, violating the Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction definition.
